body_txt: This trope is when somebody does something wrong, but it's a mild wrong, like a white lie, a low misdemeanor (as in a $20 fine), or something that at most gets a "Hey! Not cool!" from your friends, and then the show treats it as crossing the Moral Event Horizon or at least coming dangerously close. That lollipop you shoplifted? Those 50 cents it cost will land you about 50 years in prison! You scumbag . How could you? The United Supermarket Corporation will starve because of you! How do you sleep at night, knowing what you've done!? This trope comes in 3 varieties: Since laws all over the world are rife with Values Dissonance , things that are misdemeanors or not even illegal in one country but treated as serious crimes in others can seem like this. Compare with: Contrast with: No relation to the notion of a "felony/misdemeanor" note a crime which can be classified as either a misdemeanor or a felony, depending on the circumstances; sometimes the circumstance is you've committed certain other crimes already, and what would otherwise be a misdemeanor is now a felony because of it. in some real-life jurisdictions, such as California. May precipitate a warning of Punishment Pending .
